description:
If the auditd daemon is configured to use the augenrules program to read audit rules during daemon startup (the default), add the following lines to a file with suffix .rules in the directory /etc/audit/rules.d: 
-w /etc/selinux/ -p wa -k MAC-policy
If the auditd daemon is configured to use the auditctl utility to read audit rules during daemon startup, add the following lines to /etc/audit/audit.rules: 
-w /etc/selinux/ -p wa -k MAC-policy
rationale:
The system's mandatory access policy (SELinux) should not be
arbitrarily changed by anything other than administrator action. All changes to
MAC policy should be audited.
ocil_clause: the system is not configured to audit attempts to change files within the /etc/selinux directory
ocil:
Verify Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9 generates audit records for all events that affect "/etc/selinux/" with the following command:

$ sudo auditctl -l | grep /etc/selinux/

-w /etc/selinux/ -p wa -k MAC-policy
title: Record Events that Modify the System's Mandatory Access Controls (/etc/selinux)
